summary: Deterministic proof that raw claim candidates are curated through user-facing and maintainer-facing canonical indexes before HITL continuation.

evidenceScope.claimBoundary: Only the docs/contracts/claim-discovery-workflow.md claim that overly granular raw candidates should route through two canonical claim indexes before HITL is marked satisfied.
notClaimed: Every canonical mapping is semantically final without sampling.
HITL review has already approved all mapped claims.
The canonical indexes replace source claim packets.
Consumer repos inherit Cautilus-specific claim ordering without their own adapter and source docs.
Claim review or eval execution is part of this curation proof.

protectedBehaviors: canonical map builder maps raw user and maintainer claims to canonical catalogs
canonical map preserves absorbed raw claim ids and fingerprints
low-confidence claims remain flagged for semantic review
user catalog parsing reads same-directory user spec pages
maintainer catalog parsing reads same-directory contract spec pages
curation-flow audit passes only when packet and both canonical spec indexes are inspected before HITL
curation-flow audit fails when the maintainer-facing index is skipped or review launches before the boundary

decision.reason: The canonical map builder consumes raw claim packets and the two canonical spec indexes, preserves absorbed raw ids and fingerprints, and the curation-flow audit enforces that both indexes are inspected before HITL or review continuation.
